summary: DHH promotes a proposed full-day session called 'The Building of Basecamp' where he and Jason Fried would share the story behind building Basecamp. The post frames the product's development as rich with lessons spanning design, programming, collaboration, and business friendship. He gauges interest from the community, noting he would fly from Copenhagen to Chicago if enough people sign up. This is an early-stage promotional post from 2004, shortly after Basecamp's initial launch.
key_points: Basecamp's creation involved lessons in design, programming, collaboration, and experimentation
Jason Fried proposed a full-day session to share the behind-the-scenes story of building Basecamp
The post is an early example of 37signals' community-driven approach to gauging demand before committing
DHH signals his personal investment by offering to fly from Copenhagen to Chicago for the event
money_quotes: The story behind Basecamp touches on everything from design to programming, from collaboration to experimentation, from blurred borders of competencies to friendship in business.
There's a lot of lessons to be extracted and inspiration to be harvested from examining the whole process.
key_insight: Even in 2004, DHH and 37signals were treating the process of building software as a story worth sharing, laying the groundwork for their later influence on startup culture and product development philosophy.
